subject: Sociology
title: The Working Class
description: A fast breakdown of The Working Class for builders.

hook.text: He builds houses he'll never afford to live in. She cleans offices she'll never work in. That's the working class.

buildup.text: The working class performs manual or service labor, often without college degrees, with limited job security.

discovery.text: Unions once gave workers bargaining power. Since the 1980s, union membership has declined from 35% to under 11%.

twist.text: Working-class jobs were once pathways to the middle class. Deregulation and automation closed many of those doors.

climax.text: The working class remains essential. Pandemics proved that society collapses without drivers, cleaners, and nurses.

punchline.text: Essential workers are valued in crisis, forgotten in budgets.

quiz.question: What happened to union membership since the 1980s?
options: It declined sharply from 35% to under 11%
